QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_24

question.text: Under SCRA 1956, what is the maximum punishment for engaging in illegal trading (Dabba Trading)?

answer.text: Imprisonment up to 10 years or fine up to Rs. 25 crores, or both
Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine up to Rs. 5 crores
Fine of Rs. 1 crore only, no imprisonment
Imprisonment up to 5 years or fine up to Rs. 10 crores

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 23(1) of SCRA, any person who contravenes sections 13, 16, 17, or 19 (which prohibit unauthorized securities trading) shall be punishable with imprisonment up to 10 years or fine up to Rs. 25 crores, or both. These are cognizable offences under Section 25. **The Trap:** Dabba trading also violates IPC sections 406, 420, and 130-B, making it punishable under both securities law and criminal law. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers illegal trading and its consequences. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Dabba trading = OTC illegal trading outside recognized exchanges. Problems: no settlement guarantee, credit risk, black money, tax evasion. State law enforcement can also investigate (Section 25 SCRA — cognizable offences).
